ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-16-2023 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-17-2023 Continued To Jul-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Jason Yang appearing via zoom for Kimfung Tang (Petitioner).

Mr. Yang stated that the reasons for the non-appearance at the last hearing are stated in the declaration. The Court finds no good cause for the non-appearance and issues sanctions in the amount of $250.00 against Andrew H. Dai payable within 10 days of the order. Mr. Yang stated the petitioner will go ahead and withdraw the disclaimer because they think it will cause additional issues. Mr. Yang also explained how the account in the uncle's name was transferred to the descendant. The Petition for spousal property is under submission with the intent to grant with limited language with regard to only the property that is associated with the decedents tax identification number.

Judge; Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Cristobal; Not reported.

May 17, 2023:
Upon further review, the matter is continued to July 18, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the petitioner to clarify her/his requests.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Continued To May-16-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Petitioner Kimfung Tang and the Attorney Andrew Dai to appear on May 16, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against each of them for failure to appear. The Spousal Property Petition is also continued to May 16, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Deputy: G. Tan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Continued To Jul-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Yang, via Zoom, for Kimfung Tai, Petitioner.

The court advises counsel that it is not prudent to forward a proposed order without attachments the day prior to the hearing. Counsel reports of not receiving probate notes. The court confirms Mr. Dai's email address with counsel. The court and counsel discuss the equity in the bagel shop prior to 10/01/2011. Counsel reports that the business was established prior the marriage and the remaining equity purchased after being married. Counsel reports that the decedent's mother lives with the petitioner and of a predeceased brother who had no issues. The court advises counsel to focus on clearing the probate notes and points to sections 7a and 7b of the petition and including attachments 5a and 5b to the proposed order. Counsel advises of the intent to file a second amended petition and a small estate affidavit. The court reminds counsel that this matter needs to move forward, or the parties will need to file an estate petition.

The matter is continued to Jul-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.
